sectionOrderRules: Hero must state the type of service business and include the primary contact CTA.
Service summary must highlight concrete recurring or one-off services relevant to property care or green area maintenance.
Trust proof must appear before the final CTA and should use real signals such as insurance, collective agreement or reference assignments when available.
Contracts summary may appear before trust proof when the business mainly sells recurring service agreements.
Sections should avoid repeating the same promise in multiple blocks.
Services intro must connect the company to practical property-related support services.
Service list items must be specific, such as trappstädning, kontorsstädning, fastighetsskötsel, gräsklippning, häckklippning or seasonal outdoor maintenance, rather than vague labels.
If pricing overview is shown, it must describe how quotes or scope-based pricing works without invented fixed prices.
Contact CTA should invite quote requests for both recurring agreements and individual jobs when relevant.
Contracts intro must explain why recurring care agreements are useful for the customer type served.
Contract models must describe realistic differences such as recurring visits, seasonal plans or property-specific scope, without making guarantees the business cannot support.
Process should explain how inspection, proposal, start-up and follow-up work in a straightforward way.
Trust proof, if used here, should not duplicate the exact same review content already shown prominently on home.
Contact info must include real phone, email and address details when available.
Contact form should be suitable for quote or service inquiries and may ask about property type, service need and preferred contact method.
Map and opening hours should only render when accurate business data exists.
